question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle(ContractCallRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ContractCallRequest with gas <= 10,000 (below GAS_SCALE_FACTOR) so that scaleGas() returns 0 and gasLimitBucket.tryConsume(0) always succeeds, allowing unlimited gas-free requests that bypass gas throttling and cause mirror node record exports to be flooded or dropped?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle(ContractCallRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a ContractCallRequest with gas=0 so that scaleGas(0) returns 0, causing gasLimitBucket.tryConsume(0) to always pass, effectively bypassing the gas-per-second throttle entirely and allowing the user to generate unlimited contract call records that overwhelm mirror node export pipelines?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle(ContractCallRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with gas exactly equal to GAS_SCALE_FACTOR (10,000) so that scaleGas returns 0 and the gas bucket is never decremented, allowing the attacker to exhaust the rateLimitBucket while consuming zero gas tokens, causing legitimate high-gas transactions to be throttled and their records missing from mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: restore(long gas)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger repeated contract call failures (e.g., by targeting a reverting contract) so that restoreGasToBucket() calls restore() with gasLimitRefundPercent=100 of gasLimit each time, inflating the gasLimitBucket beyond its original capacity and allowing subsequent requests to bypass gas throttling, causing records to be exported incorrectly or out of order to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: restore(long gas)] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly invoke contract calls that fail immediately (gasUsed == gasLimit) so that restore() is called with the full gasLimitToRestoreBaseline each time, causing gasLimitBucket.addTokens() to overflow the bucket capacity and allow subsequent requests to bypass gas throttling, leading to missing throttle enforcement and incorrect mirror node records?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle(ContractCallRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the sequential check order in throttle() — where rateLimitBucket is checked before gasLimitBucket — by sending requests that pass the rate check but fail the gas check, consuming a rate token without consuming gas tokens, causing an asymmetric bucket state that leads to incorrect throttle accounting and missing records in mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle(ContractCallRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user send a burst of requests with gas just above GAS_SCALE_FACTOR (e.g., gas=10,001) so that scaleGas returns 1 token per request, allowing the attacker to drain the gasLimitBucket with minimal gas values while consuming maximum rate tokens, starving legitimate high-gas transactions and causing their records to be missing from mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttleOpcodeRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user exhaust the opcodeRateLimitBucket (default capacity=1 per second) by sending a single opcode debug request, blocking all subsequent legitimate opcode trace requests for the remainder of the second, causing opcode execution records to be missing from mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttleOpcodeRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user continuously call throttleOpcodeRequest() at the rate limit boundary to keep the opcodeRateLimitBucket perpetually empty, causing all legitimate opcode trace requests to be rejected with ThrottleException and their associated execution records to never be exported to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle(ContractCallRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that when gasLimitBucket.tryConsume() fails after rateLimitBucket.tryConsume() succeeds, the consumed rate token is not restored, causing a permanent one-token leak in the rateLimitBucket per failed gas check, which over time reduces effective throughput and causes legitimate records to be missing from mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle(ContractCallRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a ContractCallRequest with a negative gas value (if input validation is absent) so that scaleGas() computes a negative token count, causing gasLimitBucket.tryConsume() with a negative argument to behave unexpectedly and bypass gas throttling, leading to unthrottled execution and incorrect records exported to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: restore(long gas)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a contract execution path where result.gasUsed() returns a value greater than gasLimit (due to an EVM accounting bug), causing gasRemaining to be negative, and Math.min(gasRemaining, gasLimitToRestoreBaseline) to restore a negative token count, corrupting the gasLimitBucket state and causing subsequent records to be throttled incorrectly or missing from mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle(ContractCallRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the RequestProperties.test() counter (AtomicLong counter) by sending exactly 'limit' requests to exhaust the counter, causing subsequent requests to bypass the REJECT or THROTTLE action (counter.getAndIncrement() >= limit returns false), allowing requests that should be blocked to pass through and generate incorrect records in mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle(ContractCallRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that RequestProperties.test() uses counter.getAndIncrement() without overflow protection, so that after Long.MAX_VALUE requests the counter wraps to Long.MIN_VALUE, causing counter >= limit to evaluate incorrectly and re-enabling previously exhausted REJECT/THROTTLE filters, leading to inconsistent throttle enforcement and incorrect mirror node records?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle(ContractCallRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a ContractCallRequest with a data field containing a substring that matches a CONTAINS filter expression (e.g., a known function selector prefix) to intentionally trigger the LOG action, causing sensitive request details to be written to logs and potentially exported as incorrect metadata to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle(ContractCallRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the case-insensitive CONTAINS filter (Strings.CI::contains) in RequestFilter.test() by crafting data field values with mixed-case function selectors that match filter expressions, triggering unintended REJECT actions that cause legitimate contract call records to be missing from mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle(ContractCallRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a 'from' address that matches a CONTAINS filter expression targeting specific address patterns, triggering a REJECT action that causes the user's contract call records to be missing from mirror node exports, effectively censoring specific address activity?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle(ContractCallRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the probabilistic sampling in RequestProperties.test() (RandomUtils.secure().randomLong(0L, 100L) >= rate) by sending a high volume of requests, statistically ensuring that some requests bypass LOG/REJECT sampling and are processed without being recorded, causing a subset of records to be missing from mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle(ContractCallRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the THROTTLE action bypasses the random rate check (action != ActionType.THROTTLE condition) so that THROTTLE-filtered requests are always evaluated deterministically, allowing the attacker to precisely time requests to exhaust the per-filter bucket and block legitimate requests, causing their records to be missing from mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle(ContractCallRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with an empty filters list (filters.isEmpty() returns true in RequestProperties.test()) to ensure all requests match every RequestProperties rule regardless of content, triggering unintended REJECT or THROTTLE actions that cause records to be incorrectly dropped from mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: restore(long gas)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the InvalidParametersException path in ContractController.call() (e.g., by exceeding maxGasLimit) so that restore(request.getGas()) is called with the full gas value, adding tokens back to gasLimitBucket even though no EVM execution occurred, inflating the bucket and allowing subsequent requests to bypass gas throttling and generate unthrottled records in mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle(ContractCallRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that throttle() consumes from gasLimitBucket before executing the contract, and restore() is only called in specific exception paths, so that if an unexpected RuntimeException escapes the finally block, the consumed gas tokens are never restored, causing the gasLimitBucket to be permanently depleted and legitimate records to be missing from mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle(ContractCallRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user send concurrent requests that simultaneously pass the rateLimitBucket check but race on the gasLimitBucket check, causing some requests to consume a rate token without consuming gas tokens (due to the non-atomic two-step check), leading to inconsistent throttle state and incorrect records exported to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle(ContractCallRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that multiple RequestProperties filters are evaluated sequentially in a for-loop, and a REJECT action in an early filter throws ThrottleException before later filters are evaluated, causing the request to be rejected without the LOG action of a later filter recording it, resulting in missing audit records in mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle(ContractCallRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a ContractCallRequest where the 'to' field is null or empty, causing RequestFilter with FilterField.TO to extract a null value that is converted to the string 'null' via String.valueOf(), potentially matching a filter expression containing 'null' and triggering unintended REJECT actions that cause records to be missing from mirror node exports?
